office_does: A state representative writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: Four candidates compete for the open seat: school-board member Debra Long, former legislative assistant George Wagner, legislative assistant Hillary Q. Pham, and business owner Trent Maier.

candidates.summary: Long is a long-serving school-board member emphasizing parental involvement, school performance, lower taxes, safety, and limited government.
highlights: Supports lower taxes, parental involvement, public safety, and limited government [S2].

spending.basis: Limited government supplies a restraint direction without a detailed budget proposal.

experience.basis: She has a long school-board governing record.

social.basis: She explicitly emphasizes parental involvement in schools.

candidates.summary: Wagner is a Navy veteran and former legislative assistant emphasizing safety, veterans, fiscal restraint, energy affordability, and parental rights.
highlights: Supports tax and spending restraint, energy affordability, public safety, and parental rights [S2].

experience.basis: He has legislative staff experience but no documented elected tenure.

candidates.summary: Pham is an entrepreneur and legislative assistant emphasizing lower taxes and regulation, small businesses, safety, parental rights, and opposition to abortion.
highlights: Supports lower taxes, public safety, parental rights, and opposition to abortion [S2].

experience.basis: She has legislative staff experience but no documented elected tenure.

social.basis: She explicitly supports parental rights and opposes abortion.

candidates.summary: Maier is a small-business owner emphasizing agriculture and business, tax restraint, reduced regulation, energy affordability, and local control.
highlights: Supports tax restraint, energy affordability, and local control [S2].

experience.basis: He brings business experience but no documented public-office record.
